So Microsoft throws ten billion dollars at OpenAI like it's pocket change, and suddenly your Excel spreadsheet is getting sassier than your teenage nephew. They're basically turning every office worker into a cyborg, but instead of laser eyes, you get autocomplete suggestions.

Google sees ChatGPT stealing their search crown and panics harder than a parent at a school bake sale. They rushed out Bard so fast it probably still has debugging comments that just say 'FIX THIS LATER' in all caps.

NVIDIA becomes the new gold rush as everyone realizes you need their chips to make AI work, turning graphics card nerds into accidental millionaires. Meanwhile, crypto bros are crying into their worthless mining rigs wondering where it all went wrong.

Turns out teaching computers to think like humans requires enough electricity to power a small country, because apparently artificial intelligence comes with the same energy bills as a teenager's gaming setup. Climate scientists are adding 'AI training farms' to their list of things keeping them awake at night.

Silicon Valley real estate prices shoot up faster than a SpaceX rocket because every tech company suddenly needs warehouses full of humming servers. Your average software engineer now needs three roommates and a trust fund just to afford a closet in Palo Alto.

Food delivery drivers get priced out of their own neighborhoods and demand living wages, which is apparently a radical concept in the gig economy. Suddenly everyone realizes that maybe paying people enough to survive near their workplace isn't just good karma, it's basic economics.

And here we are, the final insult to injury: your burrito now costs EIGHTEEN DOLLARS because some AI chatbot in a server farm caused a butterfly effect that makes your lunch break feel like a mortgage payment. EIGHTEEN DOLLARS FOR BEANS AND RICE WRAPPED IN FLOUR, PEOPLE!
